[Home](./index.md) &gt; [@ofocus/sdk](./sdk.md) &gt; [paginatePages](./sdk.paginatepages.md)

## paginatePages() function

Auto-paginate a list query, yielding one full page (`T[]`<!-- -->) at a time.

The helper owns pagination control: it overrides `limit` and `offset` on each call (using `pageSize` as the per-page `limit` and stepping `offset` forward), and keeps requesting pages until the query reports `hasMore === false`<!-- -->. It does \*\*not\*\* strip aggregating options (`count` / `idsOnly` / `first` / `last` / `groupBy`<!-- -->); if the query returns a non-`list` shape it throws a [PaginationError](./sdk.paginationerror.md) instead.

Page size resolves as `pageSize ?? options.limit ?? 100`<!-- -->. Iteration starts at `options.offset ?? 0`<!-- -->.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export declare function paginatePages<F extends ListQueryFn<unknown, PaginationOptions>>(queryFn: F, options?: Parameters<F>[0], pageSize?: number): AsyncGenerator<QueryFnItem<F>[], void, undefined>;
```

**Returns:**

AsyncGenerator&lt;[QueryFnItem](./sdk.queryfnitem.md)<!-- -->&lt;F&gt;\[\], void, undefined&gt;

An async generator yielding non-empty pages of items.

## Exceptions

[PaginationError](./sdk.paginationerror.md) if a page fails (`success === false`<!-- -->), the query returns no data, or the result is not a `list` shape.

## Remarks

This is offset-based pagination over a live OmniFocus database. If the underlying data changes mid-iteration (tasks added, completed, or deleted), pages can skip or duplicate items. This is inherent to offset pagination and is not corrected here.

## Example


```ts
for await (const page of paginatePages(queryProjects, { status: "active" }, 250)) {
  await processBatch(page);
}
```
